serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Rumali Roti is approximately one large thin piece weighing around 60 grams, typically served folded like a handkerchief alongside curries or dal.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Rumali Roti / Thin Wheat Flatbread
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Rumali Roti is made from refined wheat flour (maida) with minimal fat. ICMR-NIN values for plain wheat chapati / roti (maida-based, dry-cooked) have been used as the primary reference and cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central entry for 'Bread, Indian, roti, whole wheat' and 'Tortillas, flour' as analogous thin flatbreads. Values represent a plain, oil-free or minimal-oil preparation consistent with standard Rumali Roti. Energy calculated from macros: (7.8 × 4) + (1.1 × 9) + (52.5 × 4) = 31.2 + 9.9 + 210.0 = 251.1 ≈ 251 kcal per 100g.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — refined wheat flatbread / white flour roti
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Rumali Roti in the University of Sydney GI Database. Rumali Roti is made from refined wheat flour (maida) and is a very thin, dry-cooked flatbread. The closest analogous food is white wheat flour chapati / roti (GI approximately 62, medium), as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) and the University of Sydney GI Database for refined wheat flatbreads. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (62 × 31.5) / 100 = 19.53 ≈ 19.7, based on 60g serving containing approximately 31.5g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~180 mg per 100g); low saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Rumali Roti contains moderate sodium (~108 mg per 60g serving) and negligible saturated fat, making it acceptable for hypertensive individuals when consumed in standard portions without high-sodium accompaniments such as pickles or heavily salted curries.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate content from maida (refined wheat flour); medium GI (~62)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Rumali Roti is made from refined wheat flour (maida) with a medium glycemic index of approximately 62 and a glycemic load of ~19.7 per serving, so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, pair with high-fibre vegetables or legumes, and prefer whole-wheat alternatives where possible to blunt postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low fat and saturated fat; cholesterol-free; refined carbohydrate is the primary concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Rumali Roti is very low in total fat (1.1g/100g) and saturated fat (0.3g/100g) with no cholesterol, presenting a low cardiovascular fat risk; however, its refined flour base contributes to a higher glycemic load, so individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate portion size and favour whole-grain flatbreads for better lipid and glycaemic outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rumali Roti (Hindi: रूमाली रोटी, meaning 'handkerchief bread') is an ultra-thin, soft unleavened flatbread originating from Mughal-era North Indian and Pakistani cuisine. It is made from refined wheat flour (maida), water, and a pinch of salt, kneaded into a very soft, elastic dough that is then hand-stretched or tossed to paper-thin dimensions before being cooked on an inverted dome-shaped wok (tawa) over high heat for just seconds per side. The result is a large, pliable, tissue-thin bread that is folded into quarters like a handkerchief for serving. Its texture is soft, slightly chewy, and delicate, with a mild, neutral wheat flavour. Rumali Roti is a staple accompaniment to rich North Indian dishes such as butter chicken, dal makhani, kebabs, and kormas. Nutritionally, it is low in fat (~1.1g/100g) and cholesterol-free, but its refined flour base gives it a medium glycemic index (~62) and a moderate glycemic load per serving (~19.7), making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. It provides moderate carbohydrate energy (~251 kcal/100g) and small amounts of B vitamins and iron from the wheat flour. In Singapore, Rumali Roti is served at Indian restaurants and is popular as a lighter bread alternative to naan or paratha.
common_user_queries: Is Rumali Roti good for diabetics?
Difference between Rumali Roti and naan

ingredient_synonyms.maida: refined wheat flour, all-purpose flour, plain flour

confidence_reason: Rumali Roti is not listed as a distinct entry in ICMR-NIN published tables; nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN wheat roti / chapati (maida-based) data and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central thin wheat flatbread entries. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for refined wheat flatbreads. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact published entry for Rumali Roti was found in a primary approved source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN data for plain maida-based roti and cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central (NDB 18364, flour tortilla / thin wheat flatbread). No exact ICMR-NIN entry titled 'Rumali Roti' was located; adaptation is documented in nutrient_source. 2. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate for refined wheat flour flatbread from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008; no direct GI measurement for Rumali Roti was found. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted ICMR-NIN source for plain maida roti. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted ICMR-NIN source for plain maida roti. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted ICMR-NIN source for plain maida roti. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted ICMR-NIN source for plain maida roti. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted ICMR-NIN source for plain maida roti. 8. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as Rumali Roti is made without hydrogenated fats. 9. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based flatbread with no animal fat. 10. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients. 11. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as refined wheat flour contains negligible vitamin C. 12. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based flatbread not fortified with vitamin D. 13. Chinese names (鲁马利薄饼 / 魯馬利薄餅) are transliterations used for identification; Rumali Roti is not a traditional Chinese food and these names are not in common use in Chinese-language contexts. 14. Serving size of 60g is based on typical restaurant portion for one large Rumali Roti piece.

improvement_actions_taken: Populated all mandatory multilingual local_names for Indian cuisine in Singapore context (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).

Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit reasons in notes.
Documented all 0.0 values with confirmed zero or naturally zero justifications in notes.
Set GI to 62 (medium) using analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008.
Verified gi_category 'medium' is correct for GI 62 per threshold rule (56-69 = medium).
Used only allowed ontology match_type values: closest_parent and none.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent — all language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases.
